behaviors.description: Cache keys generated from identical query parameters are always identical, and keys from different parameters never collide.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "Cache keys generated from identical query parameters are always identical...different parameters never collide." Slot B: handoff absent (pure key-derivation logic at one function/object boundary). Slot C: no lower adjacent level exists; Unit is already the floor. Rejection gate: Integration was considered, but behavior text is strictly about deterministic key-generation correctness, not cross-module state handoff. Final label: Unit because Slot B shows a local logic boundary, and Slot C confirms no lower level.
behaviors.test_description: Test the key-generation function directly with canonicalized parameter permutations, equality cases, and adversarial distinct-parameter cases to detect deterministic/collision bugs.

behaviors.description: The TTL for a cache entry is computed correctly based on the data category's configured freshness rule (e.g., user profiles = 5 minutes, product catalog = 1 hour).
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "TTL...computed correctly based on the data category's configured freshness rule." Slot B: handoff absent (rule-to-duration mapping/calculation logic). Slot C: no lower adjacent level exists; Unit is the minimum. Rejection gate: Integration was considered, but no behavior text requires runtime config plumbing across modules; it asserts computation correctness from provided rules. Final label: Unit because Slot B is local calculation logic, and Slot C keeps Unit as the floor.
behaviors.test_description: Call TTL computation with category-rule fixtures and boundary values (known categories, missing category fallback, unit conversion) and assert exact durations.

behaviors.description: When memory usage exceeds the configured threshold, the eviction strategy switches from LRU to LFU as specified in the configuration.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "When memory usage exceeds the configured threshold, the eviction strategy switches from LRU to LFU..." Slot B: handoff across modules (memory-usage source + config + strategy selector). Slot C: if lowered to Unit, a test of selector logic alone can miss wiring failures where stale/incorrect usage metrics or config propagation prevents switching. Integration brake: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess required if memory metric is injected in-process; (b) no AI/ML output; (c) no multi-agentic chain; (d) no LLM-stage orchestration. Rejection gate: System rejected because behavior does not require real OS memory primitives, only threshold-driven module interaction. Final label: Integration because Slot B is module handoff, and Slot C is a realistic miss at Unit.
behaviors.test_description: Wire cache manager, memory monitor adapter (in-process), and strategy factory; drive usage across threshold and assert runtime strategy changes per config without relying on pure function stubs.

behaviors.description: A write to the underlying PostgreSQL table invalidates all cache entries whose keys match the affected rows.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "A write to the underlying PostgreSQL table invalidates all cache entries..." Slot B: real-infra boundary (actual PostgreSQL write/transaction behavior driving invalidation). Slot C: if lowered to Integration with fake DB/repo, tests can miss real DB-trigger/transaction-visibility timing and SQL row-matching semantics that break invalidation in production. Rejection gate: Integration rejected because behavior explicitly anchors correctness to PostgreSQL write effects, which can fail only with real DB semantics. Final label: System because Slot B is real infrastructure, and Slot C is a concrete fake-hidden failure.
behaviors.test_description: Run against a real PostgreSQL instance, seed cache entries, execute real table writes/updates in transactions, and assert only matching keys are invalidated after commit.

behaviors.description: The eviction policy removes the correct entries — least-recently-used under LRU, least-frequently-used under LFU — when the cache is full.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "eviction policy removes the correct entries...under LRU...under LFU...when the cache is full." Slot B: handoff absent (policy algorithm/data-structure behavior). Slot C: no lower adjacent level exists; Unit is minimum. Rejection gate: Integration was considered, but behavior text targets policy correctness itself, not inter-module synchronization or external infra effects. Final label: Unit because Slot B is local algorithmic correctness and Slot C confirms Unit floor.
behaviors.test_description: Exercise policy objects with deterministic access/update sequences to verify exact victim selection for LRU and LFU at capacity boundaries.
